steps.title: Read September's values
steps.content: The statement must use the September data from the graph, not a general pattern across all months. In September, three cities are below the 8-inch level: Rincon, Ponce, and Isabela. San Sebastian is the exception because its September average rainfall is above 8 inches.

steps.title: Avoid false groupings
steps.content: A reverses the relationship by saying Rincon's rainfall is greater than the rainfall in the other three cities. B groups Rincon with Ponce and Isabela with San Sebastian, but the graph shows Isabela below 8 inches rather than near San Sebastian's higher value. D also creates an inaccurate pairing by treating Ponce as similar to San Sebastian.

steps.title: State the accurate split
steps.content: The best completion should describe the visible split in September: Rincon, Ponce, and Isabela are all under the same threshold, while San Sebastian is higher than that threshold. C states exactly that comparison, using 8 inches as the dividing point between the three lower cities and San Sebastian. The answer is C.
